channel.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories
channel.description: Top stories in the U.S. and world news, politics, health, science, business, music, arts and culture. Nonprofit journalism with a mission. This is NPR.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

image.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories

item.title: Class-action lawsuit filed after the Potomac sewage spill
item.description: A class-action lawsuit has been filed after part of a decades-old sewer line in Maryland collapsed in January, sending raw sewage into the Potomac River. After weather delays, repair work has resumed.
item.content:encoded:
A class-action lawsuit has been filed after part of a decades-old sewer line in Maryland collapsed in January, sending raw sewage into the Potomac River. After weather delays, repair work has resumed.

(Image credit: Cliff Owen)
item.dc:creator: Jeff Brady

item.title: Kennedy Center president departs – months before the art complex's scheduled closing
item.description: In a post on Truth Social, President Trump announced Friday afternoon that Richard Grenell is leaving the Kennedy Center. The arts complex is scheduled to close in July for renovations.
item.content:encoded:
Melania at the Kennedy Center in Washington, DC, on January 29, 2026.'/>

In a post on Truth Social, President Trump announced Friday afternoon that Richard Grenell is leaving the Kennedy Center. The arts complex is scheduled to close in July for renovations.

(Image credit: ALEX WROBLEWSKI/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas

item.title: Judge blocks DOJ's criminal probe of Federal Reserve, blasting it as political
item.description: A federal judge has put the brakes on a criminal probe of the Federal Reserve, saying it was part of an improper campaign by the Trump administration to pressure the central bank into cutting interest rates.
item.content:encoded:
A federal judge has put the brakes on a criminal probe of the Federal Reserve, saying it was part of an improper campaign by the Trump administration to pressure the central bank into cutting interest rates.

(Image credit: Kevin Dietsch)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: A cholesterol test you've never heard of is now recommended to prevent heart disease
item.description: The test can help assess your lifetime risk for cardiovascular disease. That, along with earlier treatment for high cholesterol, is part of new doctors' guidelines.
item.content:encoded:
The test can help assess your lifetime risk for cardiovascular disease. That, along with earlier treatment for high cholesterol, is part of new doctors' guidelines.

(Image credit: ER Productions Limited/Digital Vision)
item.dc:creator: Allison Aubrey

item.title: Vaccinating bats could be good for people. But how do you vaccinate a bat?
item.description: In a new study, bats lap up vaccine-laced saline or chow down on vaccine-carrying mosquitoes. Will that have any impact on the flying mammal's immune system?
item.content:encoded:
In a new study, bats lap up vaccine-laced saline or chow down on vaccine-carrying mosquitoes. Will that have any impact on the flying mammal's immune system?

(Image credit: DeAgostini)
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: And the Oscar goes to — wait, why is it called an Oscar?
item.description: The Academy Awards officially adopted the "Oscars" nickname in 1939. But who is Oscar, and who started calling them that? We may never know. But here are four enduring legends to consider.
item.content:encoded:
The Academy Awards officially adopted the "Oscars" nickname in 1939. But who is Oscar, and who started calling them that? We may never know. But here are four enduring legends to consider.

(Image credit: Matt Sayles)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: TSA workers miss a full paycheck, while travelers keep paying airport security fees
item.description: Many TSA workers received no money in their paychecks Friday as the partial DHS shutdown drags on. Fees paid by airline passengers keep piling up, even as airport security officers work without pay.
item.content:encoded:
Many TSA workers received no money in their paychecks Friday as the partial DHS shutdown drags on. Fees paid by airline passengers keep piling up, even as airport security officers work without pay.

(Image credit: Annabelle Gordon)
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose

item.title: How Italy became the darlings (and contenders, too) of the World Baseball Classic
item.description: With espresso shots, kisses on the cheek and Andrea Bocelli singalongs, Team Italy has charmed the baseball world. But their mission is more ambitious: Turn Italy into a bona fide baseball factory.
item.content:encoded:
With espresso shots, kisses on the cheek and Andrea Bocelli singalongs, Team Italy has charmed the baseball world. But their mission is more ambitious: Turn Italy into a bona fide baseball factory.

(Image credit: Alex Slitz)
item.dc:creator: Becky Sullivan

item.title: After firings, funding cuts, and a shooting, can a demoralized CDC workforce recover?
item.description: It's been a year since mass firings began at the CDC, the federal public health agency. Then came a shooting, and the government shutdown. Atlanta is still feeling the economic and emotional effects.
item.content:encoded:
It's been a year since mass firings began at the CDC, the federal public health agency. Then came a shooting, and the government shutdown. Atlanta is still feeling the economic and emotional effects.

(Image credit: Elijah Nouvelage)
item.dc:creator: Jess Mador

item.title: 'Scarpetta' is a captivating murder mystery — and a high-wire balancing act
item.description: Based on a series of novels by best-selling author Patricia Cornwell, Scarpetta follows two different mysteries from two different timelines. It's structurally complicated — but it all holds up.
item.content:encoded: Based on a series of novels by best-selling author Patricia Cornwell, Scarpetta follows two different mysteries from two different timelines. It's structurally complicated — but it all holds up.
item.dc:creator: David Bianculli
